Meanwhile, Raj goes on a date with Lucy, who tries to sneak out of the restaurants bathroom window - but ends up getting stuck and calling for his help

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is offered an exciting job opportunity, but it will mean he has to leave the country for several months. Penny tries to support his ambitions, while Sheldon is jealous and attempts to discourage him from accepting the post. Meanwhile, Raja pushes the boundaries with Lucy, but puts their relationship in jeopardy

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard returns from his research expedition in the North Sea and undergoes an adjustment period with Sheldon. Howard experiences an unusual emotional reaction as a result of his relationship with his mother. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends compete in a scavenger hunt designed by Raj to test their scientific knowledge and life skills, but the game brings out the best and worst in everyone. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon stumbles upon a major scientific breakthrough, causing him to feel like a fraud, while Penny steps up her game with Leonard after seeing Howard make a romantic gesture to Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Meanwhile, Raja pushes the boundaries with Lucy, but puts their relationship in jeopardy

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon stumbles upon a major scientific breakthrough, causing him to feel like a fraud, while Penny steps up her game with Leonard after seeing Howard make a romantic gesture to Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: This Farming Life

Nikki and Ollie welcome their first colony of rabbit kits as their breeding programme takes off. In the Cairngorms National Park, Robert heads to market to sell last years crop of spring calves - a nerve-racking wait to find out what this years profit for a years hard work will be

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the days grow longer, the MacDonalds round up their pregnant Highland cattle on the tiny island of Vallay, off North Uist, for a pre-natal check up and vitamin boost. In the Cairngorms, Jennis new alpaca arena is finally finished, which means her menagerie of alpacas, donkeys and goats must be moved to their new home. Nikki and Ollie speculate to accumulate in Moray, splashing out on a new honesty shack.

programme.title: This Farming Life

programme.desc: Lady Felicia asks the ecclesiastical sleuth to help prove a convicted serial killer didnt kill her beloved goddaughter. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Gillian Fletcham reveals a talent scout is heading to Kembleford to find dancers for TV sensation Go Dancing, Mrs Devine and Brenda are delighted. As the showcase nears, Father Brown is shocked at how cut throat the world of competitive dancing is. Gillian dumps her regular dance partner, her brother Derek Fletcham in favour of Italian Stallion Mario Cantoloni, while award-winning couple Pauline Danbury and Robert Danbury find the pressure a strain on their relationship. The very next day, Gillian is found murdered at the dance school. Derek is firmly in the frame, but Father Brown isnt convinced and investigates the dancing rivalries. Fifties-set crime drama, guest starring Kevin Clifton, with Mark Williams

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The presenter of TV holiday series Hot Shots takes a midnight swim, only for her body to be washed up on the shore. Initial evidence points towards accidental drowning, but Jack is troubled by marks on the victims neck - and as he questions her colleagues, a secret affair, unrequited love and rocky working relationships convince the DI that this was no accident. With Florence away on a romantic weekend, JP steps up to help the DI with the case, and with the tourist board up in arms about the crime, Commissioner Selwyn keeps a close eye on the investigation

programme.desc: The chefs final trip takes him to Morocco, where he samples the delights of couscous, tagines and mint tea in the countrys bazaars, before travelling along the old silk route in south-eastern Turkey to taste kebabs, another of the Mediterraneans favourite foods

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: 24 kilograms of cocaine are found concealed in the air tank of a truck, and a cache of weapons bound for Mexico is intercepted

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers intercept a shipment of hundreds of gun parts headed for Mexico, a nurse is caught smuggling pills in her boots, and a traveller carrying goat meat is inspected

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Engineering enthusiast Guy Martin celebrates the workers of the Industrial Revolution by recreating some of the 19th centurys most impressive technical achievements. Along the way he focuses on the unsung heroes of the time - the men, women and children who worked 14 hours a day for little pay to create extraordinary marvels that changed the country for ever. He begins by overhauling a steam locomotive currently in use on the Severn Valley Railway, repairing its boiler, safety valves and one of its two-ton wheels. He also lays some track using the same methods as the notorious navvies - the notorious, hard-living labourers who put together Britains entire railway infrastructure by hand

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May put three supercars through their paces in Italy. Their weapons of choice are the 691bhp Lamborghini Aventador, McLarens hi-tech MP4-12C, and a British brute in the form of the Noble M600. The journey begins at Nardo, a high-speed testing ground so vast it can be seen from space, and includes a nerve-racking drive in Rome before reaching its conclusion with a timed challenge against the Stig at the Imola circuit

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May travel to Beijing to learn about Chinas car industry, which has seen great expansion in recent years - but also have a violent encounter with one of the Stigs cousins. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond immerses himself in the world of Nascar at a race in Texas, and the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG Roadster is wrestled around the test track, emitting serious amounts of noise and smoke in the process. Future host Matt LeBlanc is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers intercept a shipment of hundreds of gun parts headed for Mexico, a nurse is caught smuggling pills in her boots, and a traveller carrying goat meat is inspected

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Engineering enthusiast Guy Martin celebrates the workers of the Industrial Revolution by recreating some of the 19th centurys most impressive technical achievements. Along the way he focuses on the unsung heroes of the time - the men, women and children who worked 14 hours a day for little pay to create extraordinary marvels that changed the country for ever. He begins by overhauling a steam locomotive currently in use on the Severn Valley Railway, repairing its boiler, safety valves and one of its two-ton wheels. He also lays some track using the same methods as the notorious navvies - the notorious, hard-living labourers who put together Britains entire railway infrastructure by hand

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May put three supercars through their paces in Italy. Their weapons of choice are the 691bhp Lamborghini Aventador, McLarens hi-tech MP4-12C, and a British brute in the form of the Noble M600. The journey begins at Nardo, a high-speed testing ground so vast it can be seen from space, and includes a nerve-racking drive in Rome before reaching its conclusion with a timed challenge against the Stig at the Imola circuit

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May travel to Beijing to learn about Chinas car industry, which has seen great expansion in recent years - but also have a violent encounter with one of the Stigs cousins. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond immerses himself in the world of Nascar at a race in Texas, and the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG Roadster is wrestled around the test track, emitting serious amounts of noise and smoke in the process. Future host Matt LeBlanc is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.desc: Following the death of a gang leader on a nearby estate, Eva clashes with a local charismatic preacher who feels the solution to street crime is prayer patrols in the area. programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Melody helps a woman come to terms with her daughters death, after the doctor is hospitalised with gallstones. Julia and Vivien have a bad driving lesson, and Ruth is made to sit outside in the cold all night by Davey after lying to Michelle about why she missed a meeting at the Campus surgery

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie fails in her latest attempt to seduce Will, but has much more success with Mubbs. Lisa is furious to find Mickie, Donna and Jess having a party at their flat, while a patients wife is horrified to discover her husband is having an affair. Terri Dwyer guest stars

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruths relentless quest to further her career continues as she undermines Zoe by revealing she is the doctor with the highest mortality rate in the ED. Lenny has a psychiatric evaluation in preparation for becoming his sisters donor, but the session unearths feelings he is apprehensive about addressing. A teenager is admitted after being beaten up by a local gang leader, and his father (Michael Starke, who played Coronation Streets Jerry Morton) is concerned that his Olympic cycling aspirations have been shattered. Meanwhile, a newly reinstated Jeff returns to work and a morale-boosting trip to the races proves unpopular with the staff

programme.desc: A social worker comes to interview Zsa Zsa and Ben - any slip-ups and Louise is back in care. However, it all goes smoothly and Phils delighted Louise will be home

programme.sub-title: The Rights of Man (Except for Howard)
programme.desc: Hobbo tells Howard to stand up for himself if he still wants Pearl to take him back - but his wife pours cold water on his attempts to impress her. Marina decides to seize the moment with Mr Waddle, and the police pull Tom over for driving too slowly, only to end up sharing his bacon sandwiches. Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In a bid to persuade Pearl to take back her errant husband, Howards friends try to convince her he is living as a tramp - but he is far from happy at the prospect of going it alone in the wilderness. Meanwhile, Tobys attempt to regain Monicas affections is thwarted by a dog, and Marina decides to dress down for a change. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wondering if Pearl still loves him, Howard asks Hobbo to give her a peck on the cheek from him - only to become even more despondent when he sees his wife deliver a passionate kiss. Meanwhile, Glenda sends Barry off to paint a landscape, hoping the activity will distract him from his hunger - but fish and chips is the only thing on his mind. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Robert Fyfe and Juliette Kaplan

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: As Sally Wainwrights drama continues, Catherine is still deeply troubled by her colleagues death - and even more shocked to learn the killer ran over the victim numerous times. Who could commit such a cold-blooded act? Desperate to find Tommy, she visits his mother Lynn, an addict who appears to be in a state of confusion. But then the woman takes the sergeant by surprise, asking a question about her grandson Ryan - something she couldnt possibly have known. James Norton and Caroline ONeill co-star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An armoured security van is discovered at the bottom of a lake, and the team makes a link to a disappearance 17 years previously and an unsolved killing, prompting them to reopen the case. Sandra feels she is making progress after finding the murder weapon, but suffers a setback when a key witness is killed. Detective drama, starring Amanda Redman

programme.desc: Sharons husband Chris becomes seriously ill, leaving her facing a difficult decision. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph star in an episode featuring an early screen appearance by Doon Mackichan

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: In a bid to raise some cash for Christmas, Tracey falls back on her typing skills, while Sharon reckons there is a gap in the market for her own unique brand of pet care

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealers teenage daughter goes missing, while an MPs son develops a profitable sideline in forged paintings. Drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer incurs the wrath of the local golf club by declaring their prize trophy is a fake - and almost gets Lady Jane expelled into the bargain. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane, Chris Jury and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Part one of two. Lady Jane and Lovejoy grow closer while visiting a Gothic temple in Scotland. Eric falls head over heels in love and Tinker feels all at sea. Drama, with Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museums sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: DNA tests on a suspected robber prove his innocence but also reveal his father was an unnamed vagrant strangled on a tube train 15 years earlier. The team reopens the case, going in pursuit of the victims other family members - including the man they believe to be his brother - while Brian questions the homeless community, only to fall foul of a gang of robbers. Peter Davison and Keith Allen guest star

programme.title: Jonathan Creek
programme.desc: Jonathan uses his intuitive powers to help the US government investigate the strange disappearance of an alien skeleton supposedly discovered in New Mexico, which has vanished from under the noses of the soldiers guarding it. Mystery, guest starring John Shrapnel and Charlie Brooks

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part One
programme.desc: When a man is killed at her university reunion, Becks finds herself on the wrong side of an investigation. Was a friend responsible?

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: Murder in the Dark: Part Two
programme.desc: As Judith and Suzie work with the police to find the killer, tensions rise among Becks friends in the house. Guest starring Harry Enfield

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Members of an all-women motorcycle club arrive in Brokenwood for the wedding of one of their number, but a murder casts suspicion among their ranks and throws the town into chaos. Detective drama set in a New Zealand town, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Whitechapel
programme.desc: The crime thriller returns with the first of a two-part story, starring Rupert Penry-Jones. Chandler, Miles and the team investigate the murder of a man crushed to death by stones. As the killers macabre methods become clearer, a second body is discovered and the detectives suspect the executions are being carried out by a witch hunter. However, their progress is impeded as links to 20th-century espionage attract attention from the security services. With Phil Davis and Steve Pemberton

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a doorkeeper at the House of Lords is murdered, Lynley and Havers believe the victims gambling debts must have been the motive. When a clerk also dies, evidence of a wider conspiracy involving stolen papers is uncovered. The detectives locate the missing documents, but realise they are being followed and must catch the killer before someone beats them to it

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museums sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is reunited with a couple whose daughter was born without limbs, and takes them to a specialist unit where she can be fitted for prosthetics. However, while the experience helps the girls mother to realise that she is not alone, it proves traumatic for the father, who blames his wife for his childs condition. Nurse Crane takes charge of Sister Winifreds driving lessons, and Trixie thinks Christopher is hiding something from her

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface is called by DI Gillespie to the home of minister of defence Charles Streatham, where guest Mary Sparkes has been found dead at the bottom of a staircase. Quickly assessing Sparkes has been murdered by poisoning rather than by a fall, Gillespie and DS Livingstone have to identify the murderer among the persons in the house - one is reporter Ruth Penny, disguised as a maid and looking for a scoop

programme.title: Queens of Mystery
programme.desc: Part two of two. Contending with another murder and a missing lead actress, the theatre company is forced to change the cast around, but the production is a mess. Despite Matties objections, Beth and Cat continue to pursue leads on their own

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: Daphne has back trouble on the day of her driving test, but Sam steps in to put things right. Starring Angela Thorne, Michael Elphick, Lysette Anthony and Ray Burdis

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: An invite to lunch in Cheltenham is extended to Sam, and the event goes well. However, the same cannot be said for the journey back to London. Comedy about a young couple forced to share their house with their in-laws, starring Angela Thorne, Michael Elphick, Lysette Anthony and Ray Burdis

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: To Daphnes delight Sam packs in his hobby of taxidermy, but takes up another - their new next-door neighbour Rhonda. Comedy, starring Angela Thorne, Michael Elphick, Lysette Anthony and Ray Burdis

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: Daphne confronts Sams new girlfriend, Angie gets a screen test for a TV commercial, and Nick spies on Sam and Daphne with his telephoto lens. Comedy, starring Angela Thorne

programme.desc: Daphne starts to warm to Sam, prompting him to break up with Rhonda - who does not take it well. Nick offers his article to a tabloid editor. Angela Thorne and Michael Elphick star

programme.title: Three Up, Two Down
programme.desc: Sam, having arranged a surprise for Daphne, is disappointed to discover that she is not free. So he decides to find out why not. Comedy, starring Angela Thorne and Michael Elphick

programme.title: Expedition Borneo
programme.desc: Deep in the heart of the island of Borneo is a lost world of mountains, jungles and ravines which hide a vast abundance of wildlife. Here a team of scientists, climbers and wildlife film-makers gather to explore this extraordinary habitat before it is too late, as felling the rainforest for tropical timber and converting the land to oil palm plantations threaten this extraordinary ecosystem

programme.title: The Two Ronnies

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin the Teenager falls ill and Tim Nice-But-Dim meets a kindred spirit in Dick Nice-But-Thick. Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Bank Holiday Monday sees the Essex contingent arrive for a sunny day at the beach in Barry. Gavin has a heart-to-heart with his dad, which gives him much to think about, while a dark cloud looms on the horizon when Dave arrives to find Nessa and Smithy enjoying all the fun of the fair. Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones star

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary realises it is high time he got rid of his moth-eaten, flea-ridden, beer-soaked sofa of 25 years - leading to a trip down memory lane reliving his youth, his first day at work and his meeting with Dorothy. Tony makes an unwise purchase when he buys a snake from a man in the pub

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffreys wife sends her new boyfriend to the camp to ask him for a divorce. When the professor is unwilling to co-operate, the pair begin digging for evidence. Classic comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin the Teenager falls ill and Tim Nice-But-Dim meets a kindred spirit in Dick Nice-But-Thick. Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they cant resist getting involved with Thomass tale as well

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie puts Marlene under pressure to fit in with the locals, so she decides to take up cookery - an idea that does not go down well with her family. She orders her husband to pick blackberries for a pie, causing him to inadvertently trespass on the land of his anti-English neighbour. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher and the rest of Slades inmates take advantage of the relaxed atmosphere - and of Barrowclough - with Mackay away on holiday. Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale star

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Parks Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supply runs out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a space rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Parks Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Parks Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a cereal killer is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell, but Gromit is more concerned that his master might be the killers next victim

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A glamorous perfume saleswoman arrives on the shop floor, prompting a disgruntled Mrs Slocombe to plot the newcomers downfall. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.sub-title: How to Go on Holiday without Really Trying
programme.desc: Hyacinth makes expensive vacation plans and discovers that voluntary church cleaning is certainly no holiday. Meanwhile, Rose ends up in a compromising position. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Mary Millar

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Whos That Merry Man with Billy, Then?
programme.desc: After recruitment for his Merry Men hits an all-time low, a letter from an enthusiast with excellent credentials gets Billy excited again, and he organises a committee to greet the newest member. Long-running comedy, starring Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lenny hears a voice from above, and decides he must wait patiently for more information, leaving the gang to make sure he is well-equipped to receive messages. Comedian Bobby Ball returns as the gullible pickle factory worker, with Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Private Pike receives his call-up papers and unexpectedly passes his medical with flying colours, much to the horror of his mollycoddling mother. Classic comedy first aired in 1975, starring Ian Lavender, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: The members of Walmington-on-Seas Home Guard platoon are excited when they hear they have been selected to appear in an army training film - but Captain Mainwaring refuses to take part when he finds out he and his men are to play Nazi soldiers. Classic comedy first aired in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted faces the potentially awkward task of hiring an X-rated film for the artistically inclined male campers. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A glamorous perfume saleswoman arrives on the shop floor, prompting a disgruntled Mrs Slocombe to plot the newcomers downfall. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: An audition gives Raquel the chance of a lifetime - and perhaps more importantly, an excuse to get out of Nelson Mandela House. Del-Boy is as excited as she is about the prospect, but only because he sees himself as the man behind her rapid rise to stardom. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del becomes a showbiz entrepreneur after discovering a sensational Tom Jones-style singer at a local club. Teaming him up with Raquel, he wastes no time in booking the duo at a birthday party for the elderly mother of a local hoodlum - but it soon becomes clear his new signing is not everything he is cracked up to be. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones, with a guest appearance by Philip Pope

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del is invited to a school reunion at the Nags Head organised by old adversary Roy Slater (Jim Broadbent), who has recently been released from prison and claims to have turned over a new leaf. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: He Aint Heavy, Hes My Uncle
programme.desc: Uncle Albert falls victim to muggers, so Del-Boy resolves to take the law into his own hands, while Rodney becomes a director in Trotters Independent Traders - and his first job is to give the car a good clean. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Three Men, a Woman and a Baby
programme.desc: Rodney and Albert begin to worry the flat will get overcrowded as the birth of Del-Boy Jr approaches, while Del feels well prepared for fatherhood thanks to a stint at antenatal classes with Raquel. Starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The couple announce their engagement, prompting the Shipmans to throw a party in Billericay. Even Bryns decision to use his sat-nav for the journey fails to dampen their spirits, and everything goes swimmingly until the conversation turns to money - and the sweethearts drop a bombshell. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Everyone gathers in Billericay as Pete and Dawn prepare to renew their wedding vows. Gavin feels uncomfortable about Gwen and Bryn knowing the details of his sex life, and Dave hears about the shocking events of the curry night. Meanwhile, Smithy goes beyond the call of friendship when he makes a staggering suggestion

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy becomes a yuppie, complete with red braces, Filofax and chrome briefcase. Meanwhile, Rodney begins attending a computer course at night school, where he meets Cassandra. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dodgy dealer Del gets his hands on a consignment of self-inflating rubber dolls, which turn out to be filled with explosive gas. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dels beloved mum apparently makes contact during a seance at the Nags Head, inspiring him to visit the doctor to seek treatment for stomach cramps. Admitted to hospital for tests, he fears there is something seriously wrong with him - but a bacon sandwich and an alcoholic drink smuggled in by Rodney soon put the smile back on his face. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary realises it is high time he got rid of his moth-eaten, flea-ridden, beer-soaked sofa of 25 years - leading to a trip down memory lane reliving his youth, his first day at work and his meeting with Dorothy. Tony makes an unwise purchase when he buys a snake from a man in the pub

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffreys wife sends her new boyfriend to the camp to ask him for a divorce. When the professor is unwilling to co-operate, the pair begin digging for evidence. Classic comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin the Teenager falls ill and Tim Nice-But-Dim meets a kindred spirit in Dick Nice-But-Thick. Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they cant resist getting involved with Thomass tale as well

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie puts Marlene under pressure to fit in with the locals, so she decides to take up cookery - an idea that does not go down well with her family. She orders her husband to pick blackberries for a pie, causing him to inadvertently trespass on the land of his anti-English neighbour. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest. They include a family of 12 in Colorados Rocky Mountains, who have isolated themselves for the past decade

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Basildon, Essex, to meet nine-year-old Brandon, who suffers a severe form of epilepsy and can have up to 30 seizures a day. He requires 24-hour care and mum Jenny cant let him out of her sight for a moment - and to make matters worse, the family home simply isnt safe enough for the youngster. Luckily, the local tradesmen have heard of the familys plight and arrive in force to help Nick and the team transform the home, with designer Naomi Cleaver on hand to ensure they dont stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Swansea to meet 15-year-old Jack, who suffers from a rare form of muscular dystrophy that has led to a lifetime of painful operations. His mother Helen and older brother Daniel do all they can to help but as he grows it has become impossible to get him upstairs and now he has to sleep in the living room, leaving the rest of the family with nowhere to socialise. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their three-bedroom semi, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they dont stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Basildon, Essex, to meet nine-year-old Brandon, who suffers a severe form of epilepsy and can have up to 30 seizures a day. programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A massive explosion demolishes a block of flats and the team is called in to help. HART are on standby as a man with a gun is involved in a standoff with police

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Miami Mega Jail
programme.desc: Part one of two. The contemplative reporter examines life in Miami County Jail, where most of the inmates are awaiting trial. He begins by exploring the large cage-like dwellings on the fifth and sixth floors, which are home to many of the most volatile prisoners. The inmates lives are governed by a gladiatorial code, leading to fights for food and status - a situation the guards say they are powerless to address

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Miami Mega Jail
programme.desc: Part two of two. The reporter goes deeper into the jail system, meeting an alleged triple murderer facing a possible death sentence. He also follows a group of younger convicts who have avoided prison by pleading guilty and attending a four-month military-style boot camp. Among them is a 14-year-old boy who may face a 10-year sentence for armed robbery if he cannot endure the harsh training and rehabilitation programme

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: An asthmatic deteriorates rapidly in the ambulance, and the crews are called to a 30-year-old car thats been driven into a wall by a 90-year-old driver

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The West Midlands crews treat a pensioner whose heart rate is so erratic they are worried he might have a heart attack on the way to the hospital

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics blue light it to a man who had a bad fall and has a suspected spinal injury, while Ash and Craig help a 12-year-old boy who has been attacked at his local fun fair

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The babys body is found and evidence stacks up against the nannys boyfriend, but Sams gentle interrogation of the parents produces another startling suspect. Eventually, toxicology reports on the victim present a stark truth demanding all the DIs sensitivity and tact as she helps the grieving mother face up to what happened

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a talented young athlete is found battered to death in a park, officers interrogate fellow guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening. His schoolmates suggest gatecrashers bringing drugs into the house may have been responsible, but their versions of events reveal inconsistencies and suggest a completely different explanation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton and Meadows play a psychological game, putting pressure on the teenage partygoers by staging a reconstruction and holding a press conference to rattle the composure of the suspects - aiming to uncover the truth about the death of the young athlete. Eddie finds a piece of wood which he believes is the murder weapon, but the nail that caused the puncture wound in the victims spine is missing

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of three. Human remains found buried in a park turn out to be those of a student who went missing 20 years previously. Suspicion immediately falls on her boyfriend and a couple they were close to in their teens. The case brings Heaton face to face with an old adversary, raising the possibility that the man he sent down for murder in the 1980s may in fact be a serial killer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The discovery of two more bodies convinces Heaton his suspect is a serial killer, but the man he sent to prison 20 years before sticks to his story that he is innocent. A retired colleague who worked on the original case reveals that vital evidence was withheld at the time of the trial and the pressure mounts to re-examine the prosecution. Bad Girls James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didnt give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called Wagatha Christie, which followed accuisations between footballers wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Dave alerts the team to a 1979 Lotus Esprit S3. Its an expensive buy and a serious risk, but its also a thoroughbred classic in need of a proper recommission

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil finds a pinball machine that should be simple to repair, but just when Rob thinks hes got the flippers flipping, another tricky obstacle gets in the way

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil relives his childhood memories with a classic Scalextric, but its a new challenge for Rob who never owned one as a child and has never fixed one either

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michaels final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex. At Cowley, he marvels at an automated state-of-the-art production line that produces 1,000 Minis a day. Michael probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford and is delighted to find that cars for export are loaded in Italian Job style on to trains at the factorys own railhead

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers and in Henley-on-Thames he slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance for Michael to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore Londons new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a 1970s beige with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: In Kursk, a battlefield the size of Wales, the tide turns, and the Soviets push back, but as Germany retreats, they destroy all evidence of their genocidal activities

programme.title: Churchills Bid to Abolish Britain
programme.desc: The story of how Winston Churchill tried to save France and destroy Hitler by destroying Britain and creating the Franco-British Union - a plan that was to crash and burn

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the grounds of Aston Hall in Birmingham, joined by Benjamin Zephaniah, who shares his passion for unusual banknotes and currency - including a gold medal honouring Ethiopian Emperor Haile Selassie. Items brought in to be valued include a collection of football memorabilia, artwork and mountaineering equipment, and wooden figures depicting characters from The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Dyffryn Gardens near Cardiff, where treasures include a ships chest from the Windrush, an ornate Welsh harp, a clock that runs on air, and a collection of ceramics, including many rarities picked up for just a few pounds. Plus, former Welsh tennis player Elizabeth James shows some of the dresses she wore in the 1960s

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team pays tribute to mid-80s-Midlands by breathing new life into what was once the fastest production MG ever made - the Montego Turbo

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. programme.desc: Robbie Cummings exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom Chinea builds a drift car with engineer Sam Lovegrove. At home, he gets a digger in to make his new pond, and he and Sam have a go at gig rowing after fixing the club trailer in Hayle

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. In the first episode, David takes a look at wheeled vehicles from the Penny Farthing bicycle to Stephensons rocket - as well as being reunited with an original Reliant Regal Supervan, as used in Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul is picking up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, inspired by American design, the car changed the face of British motoring

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil finds a pinball machine that should be simple to repair, but just when Rob thinks hes got the flippers flipping, another tricky obstacle gets in the way

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil relives his childhood memories with a classic Scalextric, but its a new challenge for Rob who never owned one as a child and has never fixed one either

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: While updating a vintage aluminium boat, the team disagree over what size and shape it needs to be. Plus, Ivan and Louiza reinvent a cycle sidecar

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgows Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michaels final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey from the Midlands to Rye in East Sussex. At Cowley, he marvels at an automated state-of-the-art production line that produces 1,000 Minis a day. Michael probes the pre-First World War origins of the Morris Oxford and is delighted to find that cars for export are loaded in Italian Job style on to trains at the factorys own railhead

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers and in Henley-on-Thames he slips into a launch to hear about the world-famous regatta. Afternoon tea on the bank offers a chance for Michael to hear about four heroes of early 20th-century literary classic The Wind in the Willows

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore Londons new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.desc: Daves drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a 1970s beige with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Dyffryn Gardens near Cardiff, where treasures include a ships chest from the Windrush, an ornate Welsh harp, a clock that runs on air, and a collection of ceramics, including many rarities picked up for just a few pounds. programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has a three-hour drive in a 1960s bus that arrived in the UK after a life in the sun. Dave takes a Triumph TR7 convertible to auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek inspects a brace of old cop cars including a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine, and a former Mod waves ciao to his collection of 1960s Italian scooters

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Goodwin and Makori work through the night to save a toddler diagnosed with meningitis, while a dying mother worries that her alcoholic husband will not be able to care for their children after she is gone. Meanwhile, Nkeshis future may be decided by a job offer in Cameroon

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, suitors Billy, Louis and Peter suitors buy an outfit for singleton Allissia. The aspiring model then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes they liked best

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: Following some of European footballs top officials as they adapt to the introduction of VAR, giving their frank assessments of some of the most-talked-about decisions

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the grand final of the celebrity cookery series, as the three remaining stars cook the recipe their chef mentor is best known for. This will be followed by their toughest challenge yet - producing their very own signature dish. The stakes are high, and only one will be crowned this years winner

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former football player and manager, who talks to Tommy Martin about his life as well as his sporting career

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former football player and manager, who talks to Tommy Martin about his life as well as his sporting career

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Chinook, a fluffy Samoyed dog, is brought in to see if the tumour on his liver is operable. Mirabella the cat arrives for a radical radium treatment that only the vets can provide in a last-ditch effort to treat her thyroid issues. Ruas owner is concerned he may have epilepsy, while a bull comes to the hospital for a delicate operation

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: An investigation into the Loch Ness Monster in which extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets out to examine the evidence, review famous sightings and photographs and talk to eyewitnesses, as he researches the real creature that lies behind the myth

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as Carol Vorderman, Katherine Ryan, Ellie Simmonds and Pasha Kovalev compete in cooking battles, taking on their favourite global cuisines and fighting for a place in the grand final

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Trevor from Northwich, Emily from Glasgow, Mark from St Helens and Viv from Fareham pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: Following some of European footballs top officials as they adapt to the introduction of VAR, giving their frank assessments of some of the most-talked-about decisions

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager opens up about life as a manager, taking the Ireland job twice, the 2002 World Cup, and the infamous relationship with Roy Keane

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the animals that thrive in inner-city environments across the country. Urban foxes did not exist in Britain until the 1930s, but now these animals inhabit 90% of towns and cities and take residence in built-up living quarters. Meanwhile, cameras observe families of hedgehogs thriving in unkempt gardens, and peregrine falcons nesting in some on Londons tallest buildings

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian returns to Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London, meeting Spencer the stray beagle, who is consigned to quarantine with a contagious virus and petrified of humans. Paul also meets Lydia, the Hungarian Puli, a heavily dreadlocked rare pedigree, who is struggling in the hot summer heat, and Tintin the tearaway Yorkshire terrier who he takes for a trip to the hairdressers

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian meets three dogs in need of companionship. First up is excitable 10-month-old mongrel Blake, who loves playing with his toys - but when Paul suspects his eyesight is not as it should be and the vets confirm his fears, the presenter is given the challenge of finding the canine a suitable friend to act as his very own guide dog. Paul also meets Jacko the Jack Russell, who has been down in the dumps ever since arriving at Battersea, and 14-week-old Saluki Forest, who is in quarantine due to a stomach bug. Paul tries to come up with a way of keeping the puppy entertained - but with no physical contact allowed, that proves easier said than done

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Ormerod finally accepts that wife Caroline isnt going to emerge from her coma and decides to move on. A married couple face the truth about their future, and Dr Weatherill is forced to break a distressing diagnosis to a patient, while Kens organisational skills ensure the charity ball goes with a bang. Starring Michael Starke, Amy Robbins and Robert Daws, and featuring a guest appearance by Tricia Penrose in her Heartbeat guise of Gina Ward

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A student nurse causes quite a stir when she arrives in the middle of the night, while Ken and Alun contend with a farming family suffering a crisis. Brigid and Jill deliver a Downs syndrome baby whose parents dont want anything to do with it, and Lizzies crash diet has alarming consequences. Starring Wendy Craig, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former football player and manager, who talks to Tommy Martin about his life as well as his sporting career

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2002, the year of Roy Keanes famous spat with Mick McCarthy, igniting a national debate that continues to this day. Bertie Ahern led Fianna Fail to victory in the general election but fell just short of an overall majority and a levy of 15 per cent was introduced on plastic bags

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Headline stories from 2003, accompanied by the music of the time

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Chinook, a fluffy Samoyed dog, is brought in to see if the tumour on his liver is operable. Mirabella the cat arrives for a radical radium treatment that only the vets can provide in a last-ditch effort to treat her thyroid issues. Ruas owner is concerned he may have epilepsy, while a bull comes to the hospital for a delicate operation

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2006

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories looking back at the headlines of the past, focusing here on 2007

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The former Republic of Ireland midfielder talks about his life in professional football and his battles with addiction

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: As storms close runways, a man has an apparent heart attack on an incoming flight. Can the plane land? Later, a fire drill turns into a real emergency

programme.title: Generation Dating
programme.desc: Two strangers with a substantial age gap are brought together, and each is challenged to find a suitable date for the other

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: LA
programme.sub-title: A Game of Cat and House
programme.desc: Altman mentors a new broker who once took a chance on him, and will Tracy live to regret her decision when she brings Flagg in on a deal?

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: A fresh batch of singles prepare for the summer of a lifetime in the ultimate search for love, with host Maya Jama overseeing proceedings at the villa in Majorca

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: Teresa throws a party that instantly becomes drama-filled when an unsettling video leaks about Luis. Margaret reveals a shocking secret about Jennifer to the ladies

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Marcos notices the competition unfolding between the junior stews and he alerts Daisy. and Tom gets some devastating news from home

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Demanding charter guests continue to keep the crew on their toes, and chef Marcos remains determined to impress them

programme.title: Law and Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A high-school principal catches a pupil sexting and the team uncovers a sinister case of distribution concerning hundreds of underage students. Silva pleads for leniency for a suspect she believes is innocent

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A chilling event at the Royal Edward hotel on the night of Assistant Chief Constable Alison Vospers leaving do forces Grace and Branson into a difficult position. They must investigate all guests, including high ranking East Sussex police attendees. Something about the incident is causing a deep unease in Grace - he knows hes seen this before. Has a sinister offender from the crime busters past resurfaced or is this a copycat? Return of the crime drama based on the books by Peter James, starring John Simm

programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Time Is Money
programme.desc: Sara Davies hosts the quiz in which contestants are given their prize money before they have answered a question, and must race against time to keep hold of it. Each player stands before an hourglass-shaped money timer that begins to drain as the questions are asked - and the only way of stopping it is to answer correctly. Once the money is gone, the contestant is out - and only one can make the final to play for the amount they have managed to save

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present more stories of people who were abandoned as babies, helping them try to find their birth families and know their identities. Heart-wrenching news awaits a woman discovered as a newborn baby on the steps of a tower block, while 70 years after he was left opposite an underground station, can DNA finally help one man find his roots?

programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Cant Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Rachels attractive and influential new friend sends Ross into a jealous frenzy, while Chandler gets drunk and falls for the curvaceous charms of one of Joeys many sisters - but later cannot remember which one

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, with Nadine Coyle, Rick Edwards and Jake Wood guessing well-known phrases for the chance to win £50,000 for good causes

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: John and Mehreen recover from their stand-off with Asadi and the Stockport crime gang and with MI5 now finally on his side, John tracks the general to an upmarket hotel. An unexpected lead from his informant Jordan gives the breakthrough hes been looking for, but he is stunned by a shocking revelation from Mehreen before he can alert his colleagues

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Alex Brooker and his mum Elaine, Jeff Brazier and his son Bobby, and Sophie Ellis-Bextor and her mum Janet Ellis compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms. From December 2016

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, detailing events from the desperate 14-day police search to the discovery of their bodies

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of more hotels and BandBs around Britain, beginning with a visit to the Rosehill House Hotel in Burnley, Lancashire. Owned for the past 25 years by John and run by his daughter Emma, they have cluttered the 31-room establishment with tat and lost sight of who their customers are. Recently, bookings have taken a sharp decline and to make matters worse Johns wife Jacquie - once the life and soul of the hotel - has been forced to retire due to illness, leaving Emma to run the place alone

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: New receptionist Ruben is about to experience his first unhappy customer as he works his first solo shift, while groom-to-be Dwain is whisked away from his stag do to get wedding-ready with fitness instructor Sarah. Also checking in today are two friends, with Michael treating his old pal JJ to a weekend away at their favourite holiday hotspot, and the pairs tickets arent the only thing he booked

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode prepares jerk chicken with rice and peas, while Lisa Faulkner makes a vegetarian curry full of paneer and potatoes

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: An insight into the tough reality of northern Jump racing, following several jockeys including three-time champion jockey Brian Hughes, Craig Nichol and Ross Chapman over the course of two days at Musselburghs Cheltenham Trials Weekend

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has his work cut out as he tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. Each question is against the clock and if a player gets one wrong they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. The first set of unsuspecting contestants think they are taking part in business competition The Applicant, hosted by Dragons Den star Sara Davies - who is in on the joke - while Jo Brand joins in the fun from Mission HQ as the rebellious guest prankster

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, with Nadine Coyle, Rick Edwards and Jake Wood guessing well-known phrases for the chance to win £50,000 for good causes

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: Wales youngest nurses continue to deal with all kinds of medical challenges thrown their way. In Carmarthen, theres no easing in for very newly qualified Caitlin who has to learn fast in her first week in AandE. Across the ward, her friend and colleague 22-year-old Mikey battles to keep a patient with severe hypothermia alive. On the Stroke Unit in Aberystwyth, a visit from Nelly the pet therapy dog lifts not just the patients spirits, but the nurses too, while nurse Gina helps keeps moral high on the ward with her funny banter

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions return to Blackpools Winter Gardens, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of todays performers be just what the pair are looking for?

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Sunetra Sarker, Matty Lee, David Arnold and Basil Brush pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr joins Claudia to talk about series two of Last One Laughing, Chase Infiniti speaks about her role in The Testaments - the sequel to The Handmaids Tale - and Lisa Kudrow talks about sitcom The Comeback. Meanwhile, Jamie Dornan previews Saturday Night Live UK, which he is guest hosting

programme.title: Tour de Fred
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix is joined by his Olympic medal-winning diver daughter Andrea Spendolini-Sirieix to take on a series of cycle rides along the sometimes challenging but often spectacular roads of West Cork. The pair visit the butchers shop where black pudding was born, learn about the history of Barbary pirates, swap pedals for waves at a local surf school and share a seaside walk with alpacas
